# Osprey: Browser Protection

> Osprey is a free, open-source browser extension that blocks phishing, malware,
> scams, and other dangerous websites before they load. It checks every site you
> visit against 22 threat intelligence providers through a privacy-preserving
> proxy that hides your IP address from those providers.

Osprey is a non-profit project. It is free, has no paid tier, no account, no
advertising, and no upsell. It is licensed under the GPLv3.

## Key facts

- **Cost:** Free. There is no premium version and no trial.
- **Browsers:** Chrome, Firefox, Edge. Other Chromium browsers work unofficially.
- **License:** GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3).
- **Providers:** 22 threat intelligence providers. 20 are routed through Osprey's
  proxy and enabled by default; 2 (MetaDefender) are off by default and require
  the user's own API key.
- **Permissions:** `tabs`, `webNavigation`, `storage`. Osprey does NOT request
  host permissions for the sites you visit, and has no content scripts, so it
  cannot read page content, cookies, or form data. This is enforced by the
  browser, not merely promised.
- **What is sent:** The address of the page being opened. Query strings, URL
  fragments, and embedded credentials are stripped before any lookup.
- **IP address:** Lookups are proxied, so providers see the proxy's IP, not the
  user's. The proxy hashes IPs in memory for rate limiting only.
- **Logging:** Safe results are never logged. When a provider returns a malicious
  or phishing verdict, a transient in-memory entry records the provider, the
  verdict, and the checked hostname or stripped URL. No IP, no user identifier.
  It is never written to disk and is lost on restart.
- **Contact:** support@osprey.ac

## How it differs from built-in browser protection

Chrome, Edge, and Firefox each check against a single source. Osprey checks 22
at once, so a site flagged by any one of them is blocked. Osprey is designed to
run alongside built-in protection and antivirus, not to replace them.

## Pages

- [Home](https://osprey.ac/): What Osprey is, what it blocks, how it handles data, FAQ.
- [Download](https://osprey.ac/download/): Install links for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge, plus developer builds.
- [Privacy Policy](https://osprey.ac/privacy/): Exactly what data is processed, sent, and retained.
- [Terms of Service](https://osprey.ac/terms/): Licensing, acceptable use, disclaimers.
- [Source code](https://github.com/OspreyProject/Osprey): The full extension and proxy source.
